options.text: The `subscribe` capability, so clients can be notified when that resource changes.
options.explanation: The `subscribe` resources capability lets a client subscribe to a specific resource and receive `notifications/resources/updated` when it changes.

options.text: The `listChanged` capability, which signals when individual resource contents change.
options.explanation: `listChanged` notifies when the set of available resources changes, not when an individual resource's contents change.

options.text: The `tools` capability, since change notifications are delivered as tool results.
options.explanation: Resource change notifications are part of the resources capability, not delivered via tools.

options.text: Resource templates, because parameterized URIs are required for any updates.
options.explanation: Templates expose parameterized resources; they are unrelated to change subscriptions.

questions.stem: The document-analysis tool returns weather-like structured records, and the team wants clients to validate the returned object's shape automatically. Per the MCP tool spec, which addition lets clients validate the structured result?

options.text: Define an `outputSchema` on the tool that the structured result must conform to.
options.explanation: An `outputSchema` lets servers guarantee and clients validate that structured results conform to a declared shape.

options.text: Expand the `inputSchema` to also describe the expected output fields.
options.explanation: `inputSchema` constrains arguments, not the result; `outputSchema` validates output.

options.text: Set `isError` to false on every successful response so clients infer the shape.
options.explanation: `isError` flags execution errors; it does not describe or validate output structure.

options.text: Add `annotations` describing the audience and priority of the output.
options.explanation: Annotations convey usage hints, not a validatable schema for the result.

questions.stem: A web-search tool returns large documents. The team debates whether to embed full document text in each tool result or return a pointer the client can fetch on demand. Which MCP content type lets the tool reference a resource without inlining its full contents?

options.text: A `resource_link` content item carrying the resource's URI for the client to fetch.
options.explanation: A `resource_link` returns a URI the client can fetch or subscribe to, providing a reference without inlining the full resource contents.

options.text: An embedded `resource` block, which is the only way to reference any resource.
options.explanation: An embedded resource inlines the contents; a `resource_link` is the reference-only option.

options.text: A `text` content item holding the resource's full body inline.
options.explanation: Text content inlines the body, which is exactly what the team wants to avoid.

options.text: A `structuredContent` field, which substitutes for any resource reference.
options.explanation: `structuredContent` carries a JSON object result, not a fetchable resource reference.

questions.stem: The synthesis MCP server returns many candidate sources, and the coordinator wants the server to hint which sources are most important so it can prioritize them under a tight budget. Which resource annotation conveys relative importance?

options.text: The `priority` annotation, a 0.0-to-1.0 value indicating importance.
options.explanation: The `priority` annotation ranges from 0.0 to 1.0, letting clients prioritize which resources to include in context.

options.text: The `audience` annotation, which ranks resources from most to least important.
options.explanation: `audience` indicates intended audience (user/assistant), not importance ranking.

options.text: The `lastModified` annotation, since newer resources are always higher priority.
options.explanation: `lastModified` is a timestamp for recency, not a measure of importance.

options.text: The `mimeType` field, which encodes a default importance per content type.
options.explanation: `mimeType` describes format; it carries no importance signal.

questions.stem: A stateless one-shot research call runs per incoming request, and the team does not want any conversation transcript written to disk for these throwaway runs. In the TypeScript SDK, which option keeps the session in memory only for the call's duration?

options.text: Set `persistSession: false` so the session stays in memory only.
options.explanation: In the TypeScript SDK, `persistSession: false` keeps the session in memory for the duration of the call and writes nothing to disk.

options.text: Set `continue: false` so the SDK skips resuming and avoids persistence.
options.explanation: `continue` controls whether to pick up the most recent session, not whether the session is persisted.

options.text: Set `fork_session: true` so the throwaway run stays isolated from disk.
options.explanation: Forking branches history into a new session; it does not prevent disk persistence.

options.text: Set `maxTurns: 1` so a single-turn run is never persisted.
options.explanation: `maxTurns` bounds turns, not persistence; a one-turn run is still written to disk by default.

questions.stem: A batch worker resumes a research session by ID from a different directory than the original run, but instead of the prior context it gets a fresh session every time. The session file exists. What is the most likely cause?

options.text: The resume call runs from a different `cwd`, so the SDK looks in the wrong location.
options.explanation: Sessions are stored under an encoded `cwd` path; resuming from a different working directory makes the SDK look in the wrong place and start fresh.

options.text: The session ID expired, so resume always returns a new session after some time.
options.explanation: Session IDs do not silently expire into fresh sessions; the cwd mismatch is the documented cause.

options.text: Resume requires `continue: true` as well, or it ignores the captured ID.
options.explanation: `resume` works on its own with a specific ID; the problem here is the mismatched directory.

options.text: The session must be forked before it can be resumed across processes.
options.explanation: Forking is unrelated; resume needs the matching cwd and an existing file on the host.
